Portal One

The Observatory

Intercepted transmissions from a mind that never stops orbiting. Click a thought to unfold it.

On driving at midnight

The moon on a mountain highway isn't a moon. It's a permission slip. Something about speed and altitude makes every thought you've been avoiding suddenly bearable to look at.

field: liminal · nightstatus: unresolved

On fog that erases everything

There's a kind of fog that makes the road ahead disappear for exactly long enough that you forget there was ever a destination. I drive into it on purpose sometimes.

field: weather · psychologystatus: ongoing

On being perceived

Every photo I take through a rain-covered windshield is a metaphor I never asked for. The city is still there. You're just watching it through water.

field: perception · selfstatus: transmitted

Portal Two

The Underwater Room

Hover over an entry. Something is always hidden beneath the surface. Scroll slowly here.

memory · ritual

The mushroom girl

There's a photo of me in a white dress surrounded by plastic mushrooms that I love more than almost any photo taken of me. Something about the costume of softness.

she sat among things
that were not real
and felt the most
like herself

home · rest

The pink room at 2am

Some spaces become different people when everyone else is asleep. A cat who trusts you completely is a specific kind of emotional information.

safe enough to close
your eyes completely
is a rarer thing
than it sounds

observation · beauty

What the gate holds

I stopped at a gate because of a spiderweb in the rain. There was a teal garden sphere behind it. I thought: this is exactly the kind of thing that saves you.

the small beautiful things
that cost nothing
and mean everything
are always right there

Portal Three

The Fire Corridor

Look up. Always up.

The body remembers.

Who are you when no one knows you?

Some nights are the whole point.

“you threw dirt on me & flowers grew, I’d be mad too”

Go Swimming / No Swimming

hold to unlock

// Same bridge. Same river. Two signs that both told the truth. I chose "Go Swimming" every time.

Portal Five

The Executive Floor

The controlled face. The curated self. No less real for being intentional.

Jocelyn · SWFL
"There are two ways to be seen. The way you want to be seen, and the way you actually are. The gap between them is where all the interesting writing lives."

Thai. Black. SWFL. Jocelyn is the kind of person who shows up fully — to the rave, to the anime arc, to the playlist that hits different at 2am, to the mirror. She watches Solo Leveling and AOT between getting dressed up and going out. She knows every BTS lyric. She builds worlds in Minecraft and in real life.

This blog is her archive. Her transmissions. The evidence that she was here, fully alive, exactly as herself.

Follow on Instagram
